strengths: Clear problem framing: truth maintenance for long-horizon agents
Well-defined method: claim schema, linker, truth-consistent retrieval
Strong ablation study isolating the linker's contribution
Efficiency analysis shows lower compute than baseline
weaknesses: Overall accuracy (62.0%) is lower than flat baseline (66.0%)
No statistical significance test for main TR gain (n=13 too small)
Method details (e.g., MCS algorithm optimality) lack formal proof
Evaluation limited to 50-question slice; full benchmark results missing
required_revisions: Clarify why overall accuracy is lower than flat baseline despite TR gain
Explain the significant drop in KU performance (75.0% vs 87.5%)
Provide full benchmark results on all 500 LongMemEval-S questions
Include formal proof or more rigorous analysis of MCS algorithm optimality claim
Add error analysis for cases where TTMG underperforms flat RAG

strengths: Clear problem framing: truth maintenance in long-horizon memory
Novel method: claim-level memory with truth-maintenance edges
Strong focus on temporal-reasoning slice, the target capability
Includes ablation study isolating the contribution of the linker
Reports efficiency gains vs. baseline (tokens, latency)
weaknesses: Overall accuracy on benchmark is lower than flat baseline
Small sample size for key TR slice (n=13), statistical significance weak
No error bars or variance reported for main results
No comparison to other temporal reasoning or TKG methods
Ablation only tests linker removal; missing schema/parser ablations
No analysis of failure modes beyond one qualitative example
required_revisions: Report standard deviations or confidence intervals for all accuracy numbers
Clarify why overall accuracy is lower than flat baseline despite TR gain
Add statistical test results (e.g., McNemar p-values) for all comparisons
Include ablation of claim schema (without edges) to separate its effect
Provide full results on all LongMemEval-S slices, not just stratified 50
Justify hyperparameter choices (similarity gate, thresholds) with pilot data

strengths: Clear, practical system design with explicit truth-maintenance edges
Strong, focused improvement on temporal-reasoning slice (+15.3pp)
Efficiency gains over baseline (59.3% token reduction)
Ablation study isolates contribution of linker mechanism
weaknesses: Overall accuracy (62.0%) is lower than flat baseline (66.0%)
Limited evaluation scale (50-question slice, n=13 for TR)
No analysis of failure modes for non-temporal questions
Relies on LLM for linking and parsing, adds complexity
Statistical significance on small TR slice is weak
required_revisions: Clarify why overall accuracy is lower than flat baseline despite TR gain
Provide more details on the 'hybrid reader context' integration
Include latency breakdown (write vs. read) in efficiency table
Discuss scalability of greedy MCS algorithm for larger graphs
